steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: Network is a noun naming the radar. Or superDARN, as space physicists like Nozomu Nishitani call it is an appositive phrase that provides an alternative name and elaboration.

steps.title: Apply the dash rule
steps.content: Paired dashes set off appositive information to separate it from the main sentence. The existing dash after it marks the end of the appositive phrase, so a matching dash right after Network must mark the start and frame it properly. Options A, B, and C fail to provide this opening dash. That matches choice D — D is correct.
